meta.designer_notes: A gritty dark fantasy economy where gold is tight, heroes are expendable, and every expedition risks corruption. Three resources form the core: gold (flow currency), iron ore (early bottleneck), and shadow essence (late-game premium).

nodes.label: Gold
nodes.description: Guild coin — earned from contracts and expeditions, spent on every building, recruit, and upgrade in this tight economy.

nodes.label: Shadow Essence
nodes.description: Crystallized corruption harvested from shadow creatures — used to craft cursed gear and power dark rituals.

nodes.label: Iron Ore
nodes.description: Raw ore from the northern mines — the foundation of all standard equipment and the Act 1 crafting bottleneck.

act_1.reasoning: Gates all equipment crafting; Forge cannot operate without ore shipments from northern mines

act_2.reasoning: Shadow Vault unlocks; cursed gear becomes necessary for shadow-resistant expeditions

act_3.reasoning: Upgrade costs spike; gold income must scale via Guildhall upgrades to afford late-game progression

flow: resource-gold → recruit heroes → stronger expeditions → resource-gold + loot
resource-iron-ore → Forge → equipment → better heroes → better expedition rewards
resource-shadow-essence → Forge (cursed gear) → shadow-resistant heroes → Act 2+ expeditions

flags.tension: Pitch states 'heroes are expendable' suggesting high disposability
flags.conflict: Standard guild economy treats recruitment as a significant investment (8-12 min earn time)
options: A) Lower recruit costs to 3-5 min earn time — heroes feel truly disposable
B) Keep standard costs — deaths feel meaningful due to investment loss
C) Hybrid: low base cost, but rare hero traits make specific heroes valuable
flags.default_applied: Option B — maintains tension between 'expendable' fiction and meaningful loss

flags.tension: Iron Ore has base_income: 0 — no passive generation
flags.conflict: Engine default expects all resources to have some passive income
options: A) Keep base_income: 0 — ore must come from expeditions or mining buildings only
B) Add small passive income (0.5/tick) from a mine building
C) Make ore a guaranteed expedition drop instead of RNG
flags.default_applied: Option A — thematically fits 'northern mines must be secured' narrative; ACTFORGE must wire ore drops

heroforge.note: HEROFORGE must price all hero recruitment within this range. Warrior: ~4000 (cheap), Warden: ~4500 (moderate), Shadow Hunter: ~5500 (expensive) per world-template.

buildforge.note: BUILDFORGE workflow inputs must reference materials in this list only. Iron ore for standard gear, shadow essence for cursed gear.

actforge.note: ACTFORGE resource_rewards[] on expedition nodes must use these bands. Act 1: gold + iron ore. Act 2: gold + shadow essence.

itemforge.note: ITEMFORGE crafting recipe inputs must reference materials in this list only. Iron ore → common/uncommon gear. Shadow essence → rare/epic cursed gear.

upgradeforge.note: UPGRADEFORGE upgrade effects must achieve these income multipliers: ×2.5 by hour 5, ×4.0 by hour 10. Guildhall upgrades are the primary income scaler.
